page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The underlying hardware is powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It includes 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions and SHA_NI cryptographic acceleration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a highly polarized capability profile. Single-core CPU performance is in the top 10% according to stress-ng, and single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks for gzip and bzip3 rank in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance is average, while Passmark CPU tests, including physics and encryption, fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model.

Database and web serving benchmarks indicate limitations for high-throughput transactional workloads. Both Redis SET operations and Passmark database operations score in the bottom 25%, and static web serving throughput is in the bottom 10%. LLM inference speeds are average for small model text generation but poor for larger models. This server represents a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-dense workloads that do not require sustained multi-core processing, such as caching layers, memory-heavy development environments, and lightweight data processing.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instructions. Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Database operations, Redis throughput, and static web serving benchmarks fall into the lower percentiles. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this configuration provides a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand continuous multi-core processing, such as caching, lightweight data processing, and memory-heavy development environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Best suited for memory-intensive caching and lightweight data processing
